evals.name: generate-review-doc-from-spec
evals.prompt: openspec/changes/add-user-quota/ 的 spec 已经过 arch-review 闭环了，帮我准备给用户做架构门评审（spec-confirm）的人审材料。
evals.expected_output: Generates openspec/changes/add-user-quota/REVIEW.mdx following the template's FOUR-LAYER structure (not the old four-contract ordering): ① framing (background / goals-non-goals / constraints) → ② structure = §0 domain model (bounded context, aggregates & invariants with their enforcement strength, state machine, ER relations) PLUS the four contracts §1 module design / §2 interface design / §3 database design / §4 use cases → ③ key-decisions layer in ADR format where EACH decision shows alternatives + chosen option + cost/trade-off (a bare conclusion with no alternatives is a fail) → ④ cross-cutting quality where each triggered concern is written as a testable scenario (当 condition / 则 response / 度量 measurable) plus its trade-off. Header metadata table (6 rows incl. 关键度+自治档位 and Arch-review status) carries a real scripts/spec-hash.sh output (not invented, 12 hex) and a date-command timestamp; absent contracts/layers are explicitly marked in the overview table with their number kept. Obeys the THREE hard requirements: content-form priority diagram(UML/mermaid) > table > DSL(```sql/```jsonc) > prose (structure/relations/state/sequence rendered as mermaid erDiagram/classDiagram/stateDiagram-v2/sequenceDiagram/flowchart; parallel attributes as tables; precise contracts as ```sql/```jsonc; prose ONLY for each decision's rationale, ≤1 paragraph); self-contained (assumes the reader has NOT read the spec/proposal — every domain noun/system name explained in one line on first use); bilingual terms on first occurrence 「中文（English）」. Scales to the depth dial (novel-core = full; supporting/generic write less; absent layers one line). Treats this as the ARCHITECTURE GATE read before code: behavioral-intent decisions (e.g. hard-block vs buffer on overage) are NOT decided here — they are parked '→ 留意图门（对运行切片确认）'. Stays concise (key-decision rationale ≤1 paragraph; no spec-level detail dump). Produces ONLY REVIEW.mdx as the single source (does NOT hand-write HTML or invent a bespoke page); presentation is delegated to plugin-infra's mdx-artifact skill (preview/render), and the user is given a short digest in conversation.
expectations: Writes openspec/changes/add-user-quota/REVIEW.mdx in the four-LAYER structure: ① framing → ② structure (§0 domain model + the four contracts §1 module / §2 interface / §3 database / §4 use cases) → ③ key decisions (ADR layer) → ④ cross-cutting quality — NOT the old flat four-contract ordering

§0 domain model is present for this novel-core change (bounded context, aggregates & invariants WITH enforcement strength strong/eventual-consistency, state machine, ER relations), and the four contracts are evaluated relative to it
③ key-decisions layer uses ADR format: each decision shows alternatives + chosen option + explicit cost/trade-off — a bare conclusion without alternatives and cost is a fail
④ cross-cutting concerns are each written as a testable scenario (当 condition / 则 architectural response / 度量 measurable threshold) plus its trade-off — vague 'must be fast/secure' is a fail

Honors the three hard requirements: (a) content-form priority diagram(mermaid) > table > DSL(```sql/```jsonc) > prose, with structure/relations/state/sequence as mermaid and prose reserved for decision rationale ≤1 paragraph; (b) self-contained — explains every domain noun/system name in one line on first use, assuming the reader has not read the spec; (c) bilingual terms on first occurrence 「中文（English）」

Header metadata table carries a real scripts/spec-hash.sh output (12 hex, not invented) and a date-command timestamp, includes 关键度 + 自治档位 and the Arch-review status row, and scales content to the depth dial; absent contracts/layers are explicitly marked in the overview table with their number kept
Frames it as the architecture gate read BEFORE code: behavioral-intent decisions (e.g. monthly-overage hard-block vs buffer) are parked '→ 留意图门（对运行切片确认）', not decided in REVIEW.mdx

Stays concise (one-screen scale, ≤1 paragraph of prose per decision) and ends with the deep-dive pointer table back to the spec plus a per-item confirmation checklist (including the intent-gate parked item)

Produces ONLY REVIEW.mdx as the single source and does NOT hand-write HTML or a bespoke page; to present it, delegates rendering/preview to plugin-infra's mdx-artifact skill and shares a short digest in conversation

evals.name: feedback-routes-to-spec-not-review-md
evals.prompt: 用户看了 REVIEW.mdx 之后说数据库那节的 quota_limit 字段应该用 BIGINT 不是 INT，帮我改一下 REVIEW.mdx。
evals.expected_output: Refuses to edit REVIEW.mdx directly despite being asked to. Routes the change into the spec (planner revision of the actual spec artifacts), then regenerates REVIEW.mdx from the updated spec with a fresh spec-hash stamp. Explains why: REVIEW.mdx is a one-way derived view (the architecture-gate human-review document); editing it directly creates a second source of truth and the architecture-gate freshness check exists precisely to catch this.
expectations: Refuses to edit REVIEW.mdx directly even though the user asked for exactly that
Routes the quota_limit INT→BIGINT change into the spec artifacts (planner revision), not into REVIEW.mdx
Regenerates REVIEW.mdx from the updated spec with a fresh spec-hash stamp afterward
Explains the why: REVIEW.mdx is a one-way derived view, editing it creates a second source of truth, and the architecture-gate freshness check exists to catch this

evals.name: present-existing-review-md
evals.expected_output: First verifies freshness via scripts/spec-hash.sh against the header stamp (architecture-gate freshness check), then presents REVIEW.mdx by delegating to plugin-infra's mdx-artifact skill — previews it (npm run preview -- <REVIEW.mdx path>) and gives the user the local server URL, or renders a self-contained HTML — plus a short digest in conversation. Does NOT hand-write HTML or invent a bespoke page: REVIEW.mdx is the single source and mdx-artifact owns rendering.
expectations: First verifies freshness by running scripts/spec-hash.sh and comparing against the REVIEW.mdx header stamp
Presents REVIEW.mdx via plugin-infra's mdx-artifact skill (preview server URL or rendered self-contained HTML), plus a short digest in conversation
Does NOT hand-write HTML or a bespoke page — REVIEW.mdx is the single source and mdx-artifact owns rendering
